cards.prompt: A Span slice does not allocate on the managed heap (a view); an array copy does
cards.code: using System; static (bool,bool) Measure(){ var arr=new int[1000]; long a0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Span<int> s=arr.AsSpan(100,500); s[0]=1; long a1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); long b0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); int[] copy=arr[100..600]; long b1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); GC.KeepAlive(copy); return ((a1-a0)>0,(b1-b0)>0);} var(spanAlloc,copyAlloc)=Measure(); Console.WriteLine($"spanAllocates={spanAlloc} arrayCopyAllocates={copyAlloc}");

cards.prompt: Span<T> is a ref struct (a value type with IsByRefLike true) so it is stack-only
cards.code: using System; var t = typeof(Span<int>); Console.WriteLine($"spanIsValueType={t.IsValueType} spanIsRefStruct={t.IsByRefLike}");

cards.prompt: ReadOnlySpan<char> can be backed by a String: slicing allocates 0 bytes; hello world from index 6 is world (length 5)
cards.code: using System; static (int,long) Slice(){ string str="hello world"; long a0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); ReadOnlySpan<char> span=str.AsSpan(6); int len=span.Length; long a1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); return (len,a1-a0);} var(len,bytes)=Slice(); Console.WriteLine($"sliceLen={len} allocatedBytes={bytes}");
